titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Harvey Lembeck Papers PASC.0287
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Norma M. Vega and Julie L. Graham; machine-readable finding aid created by Norma M. Vega and Julie Graham.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 July.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-07-15 10:48:02 -0700.

did.abstract: Harvey Lembeck made his acting debut in the Broadway production of Mr. Roberts (1948) and went on to establish himself in film, television, and on stage. The collection consists of photographs, clippings, ephemera and a small number of scripts related to his career.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

odd.head: UCLA Catalog Record ID
odd.p: UCLA Catalog Record ID: 9952203043606533

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Harvey Lembeck Papers (Collection PASC 287). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Norma M. Vega and Julie L. Graham.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Harvey Lembeck was born in Brooklyn, NY, on April 15, 1923. His numerous credits include roles in film, television, and theater. He made his acting debut in the Broadway production of Mr. Roberts with Henry Fonda (1948). His film work includes Stalag 17 (1953), Love with the Proper Stranger (1963), The Unsinkable Molly Brown (1964), and he is well known for his recurring role as Erich Von Zipper in a string of 1960s beach party films. He established his television career as Rocco Barbella on The Phil Silvers Show: You'll Never Get Rich (1955-1959). He founded the Harvey Lembeck Comedy Workshop (1964) to train performers in the art of comedy. Lembeck died in Beverly Hills, CA, on January 5, 1982.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The bulk of the collection consists of photographs, clippings, and/or printed ephemera for a variety of motion picture, television, and stage projects related to the career of actor Harvey Lembeck. Additionally, there are a small number of scripts for selected projects including, Anytown USA, Hotel or Why Do You Need Such Big Potatoes, Mother Juggs and Speed, Nice People, Man of La Mancha, and a project titled Von Zipper, among others. It is important to mention that there are no scripts for number of other projects in which Lembeck was involved, such as beach party films, Ensign O'Toole, Love with a Proper Stranger, Mr. Roberts, Sergeant Bilko, Stalag 17, or The Unsinkable Molly Brown. Additional materials in the collection include a small number of fan letters (1950s, 1970s, and 1980s); a small amount of clippings and printed ephemera related to the Harvey Lembeck Comedy Workshop; and a small sampling of photographs documenting in a small way Lembeck's philanthropic associations. File titles were supplied by the processor.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
Arranged in the following series:
Series 1. Awards (Box 1)
Series 2. Baseball Team (Box 1)
Series 3. Business Files (Box 1)
Series 4. Education (Box 4)
Series 5. Fan Mail (Box 1)
Series 6. Harvey Lembeck Comedy Workshop (Box 1)
Series 7. Material related to the Death of Harvey Lembeck (Box 1)
Series 8. Philanthropic Work (Box 1).
Series 9. Projects (Boxes 1-5)
Series 10. Resumes (Box 1)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The series includes award certificates from the Actors Fund and National Society of Literature and the Arts.

did.unittitle: Certificates from Actors Fund and National Society of Literature and the Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The series consists of one file containing seven photographs of baseball teams organized by Lembeck. There was no background information available about the teams, but it appears they include individuals from the entertainment industry. The photographs are not dated or labeled, and are not arranged in a particular order.

did.unittitle: Baseball team photographs (teams organized by HL) (7 items)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The series consists of one file of contracts and agreements for various television and commercials appearances. The file is arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Contracts and agreements for various projects

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Mostly for television appearances and commercials ads.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series contains a copy of student transcripts from New York University Washington Square College of Arts and Science, ca.1946-47.

did.unittitle: New York University Washington Square College of Arts and Science student copy of transcripts (2 copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series is one folder of fan letters written to Lembeck and includes notes from Lembeck's parents. The letters are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Series 6. Harvey Lembeck Comedy Workshop (1975, 1978-1986, 1999; Box 1)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The Harvey Lembeck Comedy Workshop started in 1964, and was born out of Lembeck's desire to create an environment where professionals could grow and hone their comedic talents. The series includes two folders. One folder contains a small mixture of press clippings, printed ephemera, and thank you notes (believed to be from students). The other folder contains a drawing by [Lisa] Leeman titled A Comedy Class, which is believed to be related to the workshop. The material is arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: A Comedy Class - drawing by [Lisa] Leeman

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Believed to be related to Harvey Lembeck Comedy Workshop.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Clippings, thank you notes from [students], and printed ephemera.

did.unittitle: Series 7. Material related to the Death of Harvey Lembeck (1940s-1980s; Box 1)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: This series includes two files. One file contains a small number of documents compiled by Mrs. Lembeck regarding Equity/Pension benefits. The other consists of obituaries written about Lembeck. The documents and obituaries are arranged chronologically.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Appears to be Receipts, contracts, statements, and photocopies of Playbills and clippings compiled by Mrs. Lembeck regarding survivor benefits following Harvey Lemeck's death.

did.unittitle: Series 8. Philanthropic Work (1954; ca. 1960s, ca. 1970s; Box 1).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The series includes a small number of photographs and printed ephemera documenting in a small way Lembeck's association with Eddie Cantor Loge B'nai B'rith, the American Veterans of World War II (AMVET), and a Columbia Hospital charitable event. The files are arranged alphabetically by organization name.

did.unittitle: Columbia Hospital Fashion Show luncheon photograph with HL

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Certificate, photographs, unsigned speech and other printed ephemera recognizing Lembeck's service to the organization.

did.unittitle: AMVET (American Veterans of World War II) - photographs and press release featuring HL

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The series is comprised of photographs, clippings, and/or printed ephemera for a variety career related projects in which Lembeck was involved. Also included is a small number of scripts for selected projects including Anytown USA, Hotel or Why Do You Need Such Big Potatoes, Mother Juggs and Speed, Nice People, Man of La Mancha, and a project titled Von Zipper, among others. There are no scripts for beach party films, Love with a Proper Stranger, Mr. Roberts, Sergeant Bilko, or Stalag 17. The series is arranged alphabetically into sub-series by project title.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Binders/covers are from scripts in the collection except for Captain Kangaroo binder. Binders were removed as preservation measure.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Reviews and promotional or publicity write ups for Lembeck and/or the various projects for which he was associated

did.unittitle: Anytown, U.S.A. - revised first draft script
Advertisement for Clorox Liquid Plumber ad - audiotape

odd.head: General note
odd.p: RESTRICTED FROM ACCESS: Use of audio materials may require production of listening or viewing copies.

did.unittitle: Command Decision - script material and program
Ensign O'Toole - clippings, photographs, and press release
George and Alana - revised first draft script

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Pilot script for half-hour daily comedy-talk show serial. With Hollywood Reporter (6/2/1978). Script binder in box 5.

did.unittitle: Girls In The Night - photographs (18 items)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Script binder in box 5.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: With photographs and clipping

did.unittitle: Hotel (The) - or - Why Do You Need Such Big Potatoes?
Hotel (The) - or - Why Do You Need Such Big Potatoes? - script
Hotel (The) - or - Why Do You Need Such Big Potatoes? - carbon copy of a script
How To Succeed In The Tuna Business Without Really Trying
How To Succeed In The Tuna Business Without Really Trying - script and program

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Script binder in box 5.

did.unittitle: I Was A Carnival Confidence Girl
I Was A Carnival Confidence Girl

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Magazine pages where Harvey Lembeck's image was used for story

did.unittitle: Advertisement for Kaluna Tropical Drinks ad - audiotape

odd.head: General note
odd.p: RESTRICTED FROM ACCESS: Use of audio materials may require production of listening or viewing copies.

did.unittitle: Man of La Mancha - photographs and ephemera

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Includes piece of cloth with [cast members?] signatures .

did.unittitle: Man of La Mancha - sheet music
Man of La Mancha - David Atkinson photograph inscribed to HL

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Appears to be cast member from production of Man of La Mancha.

did.unittitle: Mister Roberts (stage) - photographs, programs, and telegrams
Mother, Juggs and Speed - script and shooting schedule

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Script binder in box 5.

did.unittitle: Oklahoma - theater programs and photocopies of clippings

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Photographic prints for selected negatives may be found in Box 3, folder 1.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: With one slide and an original drawing of Harvey Lembeck and Allan Melvin in their Bilko roles by Joe E. O'Connell (1955).

did.unittitle: Something Old Or Something New - notes
There Is No 13 - printed ephemera for 24th Berlin International Film Festival
U.S.S. Teakettle - photographs and cast sheet
Frank Sinatra jokes - notes for bit (unknown event)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Appears to be publicity photographs for television project.

did.unittitle: Here Comes Diana Pechefsky (The Huntress) - [continuity script?]
Uncle Frank - or - Services Unlimited - treatment

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Mostly head shots with some candid shots of Harvey Lembeck. Negatives for selected items may be found in Box 3, folder 2.

did.unittitle: We Open In New Haven - photograph

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Photograph of TAV Celebrity Theater marquee mounted on wood plaque.

did.unittitle: We Open In New Haven - script

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Script binder in box 5.

did.unittitle: We Open In New Haven - photographs of cast and crew (66 items)
Willie and Joe Back At The Front
Willie and Joe Back At The Front - photographs (20 items)
Willie and Joe Back At The Front - clippings and press book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: The series consists of two resumes and a printed image of Lembeck.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Two resumes and printed image of Lembeck on cardstock.
